div.h3: Schedule Functions, Macros, and Data Contracts

div.p: Lowercase sigma uses SHR (destructive). Uppercase Sigma uses ROTR (lossless).
returns: const uint8_t * block_index × 64 byte offset into sha256_padded_t.data[] size: exactly 64 bytes order: big-endian, FIPS 180-4
Pure function: same block → same W[0..63] Thread-safe: distinct (block, W) pairs concurrent Stack: W[64] = 256 bytes, caller-allocated
W[0..15]: parsed from block (big-endian) W[16..63]: recurrence expansion Size: 64 × 4 = 256 bytes
Consumes W[t] for t = 0..63. Imports ROTR32 and SHR32 to build SIGMA_UPPER_0 and SIGMA_UPPER_1.
((uint32_t)(x) >> (n)) | ((uint32_t)(x) << (32-(n))) Type: uint32_t → uint32_t Precond: n ∈ [1..31] Property: lossless — all bits preserved UB guard: n=0 or n=32 → undefined behavior
✅ EXPORTED to M3 Used by SIGMA_UPPER_0, SIGMA_UPPER_1
((uint32_t)(x) >> (n)) Type: uint32_t → uint32_t Property: destructive — low n bits discarded Fills: vacated MSBs with zero (logical shift) Note: uint32_t guarantees logical not arithmetic shift
✅ EXPORTED to M3 Used by SIGMA_LOWER_0, SIGMA_LOWER_1
ROTR32(x, 7) ^ ROTR32(x, 18) ^ SHR32(x, 3) FIPS 180-4: §4.1.2 Eq. (4.6) Constants: (7, 18, 3) Applied to: W[t-15] in recurrence Non-invertible: SHR discards 3 bits
🔒 PRIVATE to M2 DO NOT USE in M3 M3 uses SIGMA_UPPER_0 (2,13,22)
ROTR32(x, 17) ^ ROTR32(x, 19) ^ SHR32(x, 10) FIPS 180-4: §4.1.2 Eq. (4.7) Constants: (17, 19, 10) Applied to: W[t-2] in recurrence Non-invertible: SHR discards 10 bits
🔒 PRIVATE to M2 DO NOT USE in M3 M3 uses SIGMA_UPPER_1 (6,11,25)
Loop: for t = 0..15 W[t] = (block[t×4+0] << 24) | (block[t×4+1] << 16) | (block[t×4+2] << 8) | (block[t×4+3] ) Big-endian extraction — explicit cast to uint32_t before each shift.
Loop: for t = 16..63 W[t] = sigma1(W[t-2]) + W[t-7] + sigma0(W[t-15]) + W[t-16] (mod 2^32) uint32_t addition wraps automatically.
